action.hypothesis: A soft mixture-of-experts encoder may beat the current K=128 topk anchor by giving the encoder more routing expressivity without the hard-capacity bottlenecks of grouped routing. Test the existing mixture_topk family at the same K=128 and expansion factor as a quality-first proxy comparison.
action.summary: The K=128 topk full baseline is now anchored at FVU 0.1853. The next round should probe a more expressive encoder family at the same K rather than shrinking K or spending another round on local topk tuning.
action.self_review: This is the right point to open architecture exploration, but it is still a first incubation pass, so the result may be noisy or need follow-up tuning before judging the family. I kept the rest of the setup close to the anchor to preserve interpretability.
notes_to_memory: Established full K=128 topk anchor is FVU 0.18532327562570572; future lower-K or cost-driven moves should clear that quality bar first.
Round 2 opens the first new architecture family by testing mixture_topk as a quality-first encoder at the same K=128 anchor point.
next_hypotheses: If mixture_topk shows even modest proxy FVU gain, follow with a stabilization pass on optimizer or expansion factor before judging promotion.
If mixture_topk is flat or worse under normal runtime, try another expressive family such as residual_topk or factorized_topk at the same K=128 anchor.
